bridge_note.indication: Chronic headache, worse this week. No trauma. Rule out intracranial process.
bridge_note.narrative: 29F with a several-year history of episodic tension-type headache, presenting with a one-week flare. Neurologically intact, no trauma, no focal deficit, no red-flag features. Imaging for reassurance.

tech_sheet.description: CT head without contrast. Helical acquisition, 5.0 mm axial reconstructions, brain and bone windows.

pacs.prior_description: No prior imaging available for comparison.

radiology_model.findings:
Parenchyma: No acute territorial infarct, mass, or hemorrhage. Normal grey-white differentiation.
Extra-axial spaces: No extra-axial collection.
Ventricles/midline: Ventricles normal in size and configuration. No midline shift.
Bone/sinuses: No fracture. Paranasal sinuses and mastoids are clear.
radiology_model.impression: No acute intracranial abnormality. No hemorrhage, mass effect, or midline shift.

radiologist_dictation: Agree — normal non-contrast head CT. No acute intracranial process.
